CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, datetime_value DATETIME);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, datetime_value) VALUES (1, curdatetimeoffset());
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE datetime_value = curdatetimeoffset();
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, datetime_value DATETIME DEFAULT curdatetimeoffset());
SELECT id, datetime_value FROM another_table WHERE datetime_value < curdatetimeoffset();
SELECT (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE id = 1) AS test_id, curdatetimeoffset(2) AS adjusted_time;
SELECT a.id, b.datetime_value FROM test_table a JOIN another_table b ON a.id = b.id WHERE b.datetime_value >= curdatetimeoffset();
SELECT curdatetimeoffset(0) AS current_time_no_fraction, curdatetimeoffset(1) AS current_time_with_fraction;
UPDATE test_table SET datetime_value = curdatetimeoffset(3) WHERE id = 1;
DELETE FROM another_table WHERE datetime_value < curdatetimeoffset(1);
SELECT curdatetimeoffset() AS current_time, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table) AS total_rows;
WITH recent AS (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE datetime_value > curdatetimeoffset(5)) SELECT id FROM recent;
SELECT curdatetimeoffset(6) AS adjusted_time, (SELECT MAX(datetime_value) FROM test_table) AS max_time;
CREATE TABLE complex_table (id INT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT curdatetimeoffset());
INSERT INTO complex_table (id) SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3;
SELECT id, created_at FROM complex_table WHERE created_at < (SELECT curdatetimeoffset(1) FROM dual);
